Get Me Questions

REAL QUESTIONS
YOU CAN ANSWER

Delivering you the exact questions people really search for. Includes real keyword data so you can make informed decisions of what articles to write, which videos to make, and ultimately rank for the right words.

Get Me Questions

Help people

If you answer people's questions google will love you. ChatGPT will love you. People, real actual human beings, are going to actually visit your site and love you for being useful to them.

What to use the questions for

Real search questions are not just keywords. They are content briefs waiting for someone who actually knows the answer.

Reddit content

Create genuine answers instead of self-promotion that gets you banned.

Videos

Make videos that answer super specific questions and shape you into an expert people trust.

Articles

Write articles that will rank on Google and AI will love to recommend to real people.

You know the subject. People are searching. Let's connect you through search.

Somewhere between your expertise and their Google search bar is a queue of questions worth answering. Most tools either cost too much, guess wrong, or invent keywords nobody types.

Ahrefs is overkill

$129+/month for backlinks and audits when all you need is a ranked list of question keywords with volume.

Free wheels miss the point

Clicking through PAA and hoping Google is truthful? Pretty viz, wrong shapes, no volume, and no check against what you already own.

AI makes up nonsense questions

AI generates absolute hallucinations. It is not real people asking real questions. It is some super mid kind of b.s.

You know the questions exist. Finding them is the job.

Clicking through PAA and hoping Google is truthful? Exporting a keyword wheel into a sheet you never open again? Manually searching whether you already made that video?

01

Hunting in Google

People Also Ask rabbit holes. Autocomplete guesses. Still no clear list of what people actually type.

02

Checking your coverage

Googling your channel or blog plus the keyword. Or trusting your memory from two years ago.

03

Picking what to make next

Sorting a messy list by gut feel because nothing shows rank status or real volume side by side.

04

Watching the niche

Checking who posted what, screenshotting titles, hoping you notice the gap before someone else films it.

Case study

Better Sheets went from random tutorials to searchable answers

Andrew started with feature demos. After monetization, growth stalled around $100/month. The shift: answer the exact questions people type into Google.

Within months, ad revenue climbed from $100 to over $1,000/month. Same channel. Same expertise. Different keyword strategy, built on real search data.

I di need more videos. I needed the right videos, for questions people actually search. Andrew, Better Sheets

FAQ

Common questions

Can I buy a one-time export without subscribing?

Yes. Export Questions is $20: one CSV with up to 200 question gaps, volume data, and rank status. No recurring charge.

Do you use AI to generate keywords?

No. Every keyword comes from what people actually type into Google, with real search volume. AI may help format exports later; it will never invent opportunities.

Do I need API keys or YouTube login?

No. You should not need API keys or a Google Cloud console. Give us your niche topic and URLs. No OAuth, no account access required.

Can I use this for a blog, not YouTube?

Yes. Point it at your domain instead of a channel. Track published articles the same way you track videos.

I already have a keyword list. Can I upload it?

Yes. Upload your CSV and get volume plus gap status. No need to re-hunt phrases you already found.

People are searching. You have the answer.

The only question is whether you have the right questions.
